facts: goal_validity_arbitration_v1 is safe but inert on the independent source.
The default counterfactual recovers successes but commits wrong goals.
discriminative_rival_view_planner_v1 passes plan/frame/detector substrate gates but its first evidence analyzer fails.
The failure diagnostic includes invalid/no-valid candidate pairs and cross-view leakage.
agent_inference: Candidate-set validity should be tested before another discriminative view revision.
Expanded retrieval is the next branch because it can test whether semantic memory can recover a better candidate set without terminal commitment.

action_inputs_allowed: non-GT reachability or navmesh-derived feasibility fields if available before evaluation labels
next_steps: implement expanded retrieval planner using only action-time candidate artifact fields
run Docker smoke on the 8 request_expanded_retrieval rows
diagnose candidate-set validity before detector or terminal objective
only add detector/viewpoint evidence if expanded candidate sets improve valid-candidate availability

decision.why_not_revise_discriminative_first: The failed discriminative branch often compares invalid/no-valid or non-discriminative candidate pairs; viewpoint refinement alone is unlikely to fix candidate-set validity.
decision.how_discriminative_views_return_later: Use discriminative views as identity validation after expanded retrieval produces a better candidate set.
